text.title: Vision Image Classification & Content Moderation Core Rules

rule.statement: CSAM-class images MUST be screened against PhotoDNA / CSAI-Match BEFORE any VLM call.
rule.rationale: Hashed-database checks are mandatory under US/EU law for known CSAM; VLM has no role in known-content detection.

rule.statement: Managed moderation APIs (OpenAI moderation, AWS Rekognition, Hive) MUST run before any VLM fallback.
rule.rationale: Managed APIs are 10-100× cheaper than VLM and cover common categories with high recall.

rule.statement: VLM fallback MUST run only when managed API confidence is below threshold OR category is custom.
rule.rationale: Unconditional VLM call wastes budget on the 99% confident-benign case.

rule.statement: Output MUST include a severity tier (low/medium/high) per detected category, not just a boolean.
rule.rationale: Downstream policy actions differ by tier; flat booleans force re-classification.

rule.statement: Every classification decision MUST log image_hash + model + confidence + tier into an audit trail.
rule.rationale: Trust & safety audits require explainable per-image decisions.
